translated: When John heard this, he called two of his disciples. He sent them to the Lord to ask. They went and spoke to Jesus.

ref: But instead of staying in Nazareth, Jesus moved to Capernaum. This town was beside Lake Galilee in the territory of Zebulun and Naphtali.
text: Wǝnsiǝ lǝ Nasaret, bomu údu úsu úsiǝ lǝ Kapernaum, ni umǝ wǝ nkpe lǝ Galilea eto Ditumbu kplɛ ɔflɔ lǝ Sebulon ku Naftali eto lekpake mfó.
translated: Leaving Nazareth, he went and lived in Capernaum, which is by the lake in the area of Zebulun and Naphtali, to fulfill what was spoken through the prophet.

ref: Then the devil came to him and said, “If you are God's Son, tell these stones to turn into bread.”
text: Mfó nya olontidi ǝmǝ díbǝ wǝ ɔflɔ nya ɔ́tɛyi wǝ nkǝ, “Lǝ Onanto eto Ubi fenii, tɛyi abambi nyamfo lǝ efiani didisa.”
translated: When the elders heard this, they said to the young man, "If you want to become wise, you must listen carefully to the words of the ancestors."

ref: Finally, the devil took Jesus up on a very high mountain and showed him all the kingdoms on earth and their power.
text: Mfó nya Obansam ǝmǝ lékpa Yesu úsuko kɔtini ko letaka eto disinti nya óte wǝ kawunsiǝ eto sekakedikɔ kenke ku ke diyǝnde.
translated: The words of Satan are lies but those of Jesus are true and anyone who believes in him will never be put to shame.

ref: News about him spread all over Syria, and people with every kind of sickness or disease were brought to him. Some of them had a lot of demons in them, others were thought to be crazy, and still others could not walk. But Jesus healed them all.
text: Nyamfo ǝtǝ bǝ wǝ diye ku ditiki nǝmǝ áyɛ éfia Siria eto kasɔ kenke. Nyaso betidi ákpa mǝ befimfi ba efimbi ate ate nlɛ bulǝ báboko wǝ ɔflɔ. Mǝ bǝtsyuǝǝ, awɔnɔ bua dísiǝ mǝ lǝ ǝsuǝ kakpe mǝ bulǝ. Bǝtsyuǝ kǝkpǝkasankli eto bufi díkǝlǝ nya bǝtsyuǝǝ, ani ku akpa díkpǝ mǝ. Yesu átsya mǝmblɛ kenke.
translated: God sent his son to this world to save us from our sins. Whoever believes in him will have eternal life and will not perish but have joy forever. He loves us, and he wants us to come to him and have life. He is the savior who takes away the sins of the world, and gives us peace. Jesus is the bread of life.

ref: God blesses those people who are humble. The earth will belong to them!
text: Onanto ákpo atsyɔ betidi ba laabokosǝ ǝsuǝ kasɔ. Mǝǝ ni mba luudi sɛkplɛ lǝ kasɔ kamfo eto bikǝ ǝsuǝ.
translated: Onanto went to the market to buy some fish. She will use the fish to prepare food for her family.

ref: God blesses those people whose hearts are pure. They will see him!
text: Onanto ákpo atsyɔ betidi ba eto ketu lekpa kɛnkɛ. Mǝ luunyǝ Onanto nɛ.
translated: Onanto killed a goat and cooked it for us to eat. I thank Onanto.

ref: You are the salt for everyone on earth. But if salt no longer tastes like salt, how can it make food salty? All it is good for is to be thrown out and walked on.
text: “Bite fe mmba bitǝ betidi ba kenke. Fɛɛ lǝ kamini ábɔ lǝ mmba bomǝ ǝsuǝǝ, ǝnsiǝmbofo kamini bokpe lǝ mu bio. Nya se bunsibǝ kutǝkǝ saa esoo, buwui hã ele bǝ befi mu bewui lǝ betidi lǝ bayɛ lǝ mu ǝsuǝ bafe.
translated: Children must always listen to their parents. If they do not listen to their parents’ words, elders will not be happy with them. When you obey your parents, you will live long and your life will be good in the land the Lord gives you.

ref: Don't suppose I came to do away with the Law and the Prophets. I did not come to do away with them, but to give them their full meaning.
text: Yesu esi ɔ́tɛyi nkǝ, “Biembu biǝ mbǝ miǝ maninsǝ Moses eto afi nnya ókpe, nye Onanto eto bǝnyɛlǝkǝtidi léte. Mǝmbǝ miǝ maaninsǝ nnya kon. Bomu mbǝ miǝ lǝ mbǝ mflɔsǝ nnya.
translated: Jesus answered them, “Truly, truly, I say to you, it was not Moses who gave you the bread from heaven, but my Father gives you the true bread from heaven. For the bread of God is that which comes down from heaven and gives life to the world.”

ref: You know our ancestors were told, “Don't use the Lord's name to make a promise unless you are going to keep it.”
text: “Kɔɔ bɛnɔ tsya bátɛyi betidi bǝnkǝ, ‘Mankpadi asa esi etudi kɛnyɛ lǝ nnya ǝsuǝ. Bomu yifo lǝ ntamu bo faka fǝtǝ Bonamute ǝsuǝ.’
translated: They said to the chief, ‘The elders have discussed and decided that you should not go. If you go, there will be trouble at Bonamute.’

ref: When you make a promise, say only “Yes” or “No.” Anything else comes from the devil. Revenge (Luke
text: Tǝ lǝ mi ee lǝ iyifo ee, nya lǝ mi oowo lǝ iyifo oowo ete. Lesa bamba sa enso nyamfo eto bɔtɛyii, etsyi lǝ olontidi ǝmǝ ɔflɔ. Yesu Áte Bo Nkǝ Boantisǝ Oko.
translated: If you have faith, even as small as a mustard seed, you can move mountains. God loves everyone, and nothing is impossible for Him. Jesus is the way, the truth, and the life.

ref: Then you will be acting like your Father in heaven. He makes the sun rise on both good and bad people. And he sends rain for the ones who do right and for the ones who do wrong.
text: Bǝ lǝ befiani Onanto wǝ nkpe kato eto bebi tinti. Itsyise wǝ lǝǝtǝ kofe laafa kǝtǝ betidi bua ku mba leeyifo kasɛɛ kenke tsyɛtsyɛɛtsyɛ. Nya ɔsɔmi tsya laani kǝtǝ betidi ba leeyifo kasɛɛ ku mba leenyifo kasɛɛ ta.
translated: In the morning, Onanto went to the farm and worked very hard. Afterwards, he bought bread and beans to share with his friends so they could eat together. If he had not bought bread and beans, his friends would not have eaten with him.

ref: When you do good deeds, don't try to show off. If you do, you won't get a reward from your Father in heaven.
text: “Bebe kukɔnɔ biǝ bienyifo kesumu eto kasɛɛ ka ele bǝ biyifo bilǝkǝ bete lǝ betidi lǝ bǝnyǝ. Itsyise lǝ biyifo nkoo, biensiembunyǝ kayofofo saa itsyi lǝ mi Anto wǝ nkpe kato ɔflɔ.
translated: Long ago, the elders gathered together and told the people stories so that they would learn and become wise. Among the stories, there was one about a woman named Anto who lived by the river.

ref: When you give to the poor, don't let anyone know about it.
text: Nyaso fǝǝ, lǝ awunsǝko okomboo, yifo nko bǝ fǝ ɔyɛtsyuǝ kulaa tsya embote lesa nǝ feyifo.
translated: In the morning, if the children wake up, they should greet their parents before doing anything else.

ref: When you pray, don't be like those show-offs who love to stand up and pray in the synagogues and on the street corners. They do this just to look good. I can assure you that they already have their reward.
text: “Lǝ bɛlɛ ola botoo, bienyifo fe kase ǝnǝmi ǝnǝmi eto betidi leeyifo. Baala buńyǝ lǝ Yudafɔ eto kalakatokɔ ku lǝ obia bǝ lǝ utsyuǝ saa lǝ unyǝ mǝ. Nlɛ mi bɔtɛyi sitinti miǝ, boofo mǝ kayofofo saa koko.
translated: When the sun rises, the farmers go to their farms early in the morning. The elders of the town sit under the big tree and talk about important matters. The children go to school, while the women go to the market.

ref: You should pray like this: Our Father in heaven, help us to honor your name.
text: Nyaso nnyǝ mmǝ ele bǝ beto ola, ‘Boanto wǝ nkpe kato, tǝ lǝ bakpasǝ fǝ diye.
translated: The woman said to her child, ‘Boanto is not here, go and call him to come.’

ref: Instead, comb your hair and wash your face.
text: Lǝ bɛlɛ kɛnyɛ bokle nɛ, biflǝ ǝnǝmi lǝ besadi disi.
translated: If you want to go there, you must tell me before you leave.

ref: Then others won't know you are going without eating. But your Father sees what is done in private, and he will reward you. Treasures in Heaven (Luke
text: Nyamfo luutǝ bǝ betidi embote bǝnkǝ bɛlɛ kɛnyɛ bokle. Bomu bǝ lǝ mi Anto wǝ boǝnnyǝ hã lǝ ote. Bǝ lǝ mi Anto wǝ lǝǝnyǝ lesa saa, lǝ ofo mi nya kayo. Yesu Áte Asa Itsyi Lǝ Onanto Eto Sekakedikɔ Eto Bikenyǝnyǝ Ǝsuǝ.
translated: God created the world and everything in it. He made a man called Anto and put him in a good place. He told Anto to take care of the place and not to eat from a certain tree. Jesus is the Son of God who came to save everyone.

ref: I tell you not to worry about your life. Don't worry about having something to eat, drink, or wear. Isn't life more than food or clothing?
text: Nyamfo eso minlɛ mi bɔtɛyi minkǝ bianso mi nsusu bufi kǝtǝkǝ lǝ lesa nǝ biudi nye biunǝ nye asa nnya biɔlɛ lǝ mi ǝsuǝ. Itsyise nkpǝ afo koya eso didisa. Nya ǝsuǝ eto uyi tsya lefo koya eso asa nnya biɔlɛ.
translated: God is the one who created the heavens and the earth and everything in them, both things we see and things we do not see. He loves all people. We should love God and love other people.

ref: Why worry about clothes? Look how the wild flowers grow. They don't work hard to make their clothes.
text: “Bibu bebe kase dituwinimi lǝ dimǝ laanɔ bobee. Kaanta olokotutu nya keendi kafia kefi keti ǝsuǝ.
translated: Children should always listen to their parents. They will learn good things and become wise.

ref: Only people who don't know God are always worrying about such things. Your Father in heaven knows you need all of these.
text: Asa nyamfo eyifo asa nnya bamamonyǝ lǝǝsiǝ kebu disi ketsyiko. Mi Anto wǝ nkpe kato yi nkǝ asa nyamfo kenke ebohiã mi.
translated: The elders say that if you do not respect your elders, you will not get wisdom. I, Anto, am telling you this so that you will respect your elders and it will benefit you.

ref: Would you give your child a snake if the child asked for a fish?
text: Nye lǝ ɔtɔ wǝ lɛkpɛ, wuufi owa utǝ wǝ?
translated: I'm at home today, where are you?

ref: But I will tell them, “I will have nothing to do with you! Get out of my sight, you evil people!” Two Builders (Luke
text: Mfó nya mɔɔtɛyi mǝ minkǝ, ‘Minyi mi kulaa o-o! Bidu mɛ lǝ ɔflɔ, mi babuayifotidi.’ Yesu Ate Asa Itsyi Lǝ Bayoto Inuǝ Bǝtsyuǝ Ǝsuǝ.
translated: Then he said to them, ‘Do not cry! The girl is not dead, but asleep.’ Jesus Raises a Dead Girl and Heals a Sick Woman.

ref: Rain poured down, rivers flooded, and winds beat against that house. But it was built on solid rock, and so it did not fall.
text: Ɔsɔmi áni nya ntu díyi boba bokpo, nya ufiebi kekleke lɛsɔ ɔyɛ lǝ diyo nǝmǝ ǝsuǝ ófe. Fɛɛ díamba itsyise óto ni utǝkǝ lǝ koba ǝsuǝ.
translated: If someone wants to do something good, but their heart is not in it, it will not succeed. Only when the heart agrees will the deed succeed.

ref: That evening many people with demons in them were brought to Jesus. And with only a word he forced out the evil spirits and healed everyone who was sick.
text: Se ditsyitsyo lefe léyoo, bákpa betidi kpǝ ba awɔnɔ bua díkǝlǝ báboko Yesu ɔflɔ. Yesu lǝ ditikididi lɔni ǝsuǝǝ, óla awɔnɔ bua nyamǝ úlǝkǝ lǝ mǝ ǝsuǝ. Nya ótsya befimfi bamǝ kenke mǝ efimbi.
translated: On that day, the elders gathered and brought a woman before Jesus. Jesus was sitting teaching people, and they brought the woman and stood her in front of him. Then they told him what the woman had done.

ref: Jesus went to the home of Peter, where he found that Peter's mother-in-law was sick in bed with fever.
text: Se Yesu dísu Petro eto diyoo, únyǝ nkǝ atikesi ǝmuǝ Petro eto usiǝambe kekleke, nya onlabe.
translated: When Jesus asked Peter again, he did not hesitate but Peter answered quickly, without delay.

ref: A teacher of the Law of Moses came up to him and said, “Teacher, I'll go anywhere with you!”
text: Afi eto oteasa utsyuǝ ǝbǝ wǝ ɔflɔ mfó. Nya ɔ́tɛyi Yesu nkǝ, “Oteasa, muutǝkǝko fǝ nsu kaka ke fantɔ.”
translated: When he was still speaking, they came to arrest him. Then Jesus said, "Friend, do what you came here to do."

ref: so the disciples went over to him and woke him up. They said, “Lord, wake up! Save us before we drown!”
text: Bakasebi bamǝ ésu básanklisǝ wǝ nya bátɛyi wǝ bǝnkǝ, “Bonamute, lǝkǝ bo! Boǝbǝ bukpǝ lo.”
translated: The elders took the children to the chief and said, “Greetings, chief! We have brought these ones.”

ref: a terrible storm suddenly struck the lake, and waves started splashing into their boat. Jesus was sound asleep,
text: Yanklɛ ufiebi étsyiko bɔsɔ kekleke lǝ ditumbukplɛ nǝmǝ ǝsuǝ. Nya ɔnlɛ ntu bɔsɔ kakpo lǝ ɔklɔ ǝmǝ kafo, nya íyifo fe woonya udufɔ lǝ ntu kasɔ. Fɛɛ Yesu wǝǝ lábe selabe.
translated: Jacob left his house early in the morning and went to the farm to work. When he finished working, he returned home to rest, and his mother gave him food to eat. Then Jesus spoke to the people.

ref: Not far from there a large herd of pigs was feeding.
text: Bakplako eto lekpa kplɛ ditsyuǝ ǝńyǝ lǝ mfó ɔflɔ békedi lesa.
translated: The hunter went to the forest and saw a big animal under a tree.

ref: So the demons begged Jesus, “If you force us out, please send us into those pigs!”
text: Nyaso awɔnɔ bua nyamǝ álɛ Yesu lekpakpa bǝnkǝ, “Lǝ ebola bo ǝlǝkǝ lǝ mfoo, nyalɛ la bo esu akpe lǝ bakplako eto lekpa kplɛ nǝ ńyǝ mǝ ǝsuǝ.”
translated: Then they brought a woman to Jesus and said, “Teacher, this woman was caught in the act of adultery, and the law says we should stone her. What do you say?”

ref: But Jesus knew what was in their minds, and he said, “Why are you thinking such evil things?
text: Yesu ǝnyǝ mǝ nsusu bomfo utsyǝ lekpo nya ɔ́tɔ mǝ nkǝ, “Beso bɛnlɛ nsusu bua bomfo eto okle bufi?
translated: Jesus answered and said to him, "Are you a teacher of Israel and do not understand these things?"

ref: After Jesus had gone indoors, the two blind men came up to him. He asked them, “Do you believe I can make you well?” “Yes, Lord,” they answered.
text: Nya se Yesu dísu údufǝ diyoo, bǝnǝmba inuǝ bamǝ edufǝ mfó. Nya Yesu lɛtɔ mǝ nkǝ, “Befo bɛnɔ biǝ moofo mi botsya?” Mfó bǝmǝnkli kɛnyɛ bátɛyi bǝnkǝ, “Ɛɛ! Bofo bɔnɔ.”
translated: When Jesus entered the house, the people there greeted him. Then Jesus asked them, “Do you believe that I can do this?” They answered him together, “Yes! We believe.”

ref: He said to his disciples, “A large crop is in the fields, but there are only a few workers.
text: Nyaso ɔ́tɛyi wǝ bakasebi nkǝ, “Bɛkuɛnke bia díkayo bulǝkǝ fui, fɛɛ beyifoyifo bamǝ emfui.
translated: So the elder said to them, “Let everyone bring their own calabash, so that no one will blame another later.”

ref: Brothers and sisters will betray one another and have each other put to death. Parents will betray their own children, and children will turn against their parents and have them killed.
text: “Betidi ebudu mǝ bayimi bǝtǝ bǝ balo. Nya antomǝ tsya luuyifo nko ke ku mǝ bebi. Bebi ebotaka bǝńyǝ lǝ mǝ antomǝ ǝsuǝ bedu mǝ bǝtǝ bǝ balo.
translated: The child went to the farm with his father. His mother gave him food to take along with him. He was happy because his mother gave him food to eat with his father at the farm.

ref: Because of me, you will be dragged before rulers and kings to tell them and the Gentiles about your faith.
text: Lǝ mɔ disi esoo, bookponko mi akpo lǝ Aba eto beninǝ ku baka eto ǝnǝmi. Lǝ mfóo, bɛtɛyi mǝ ku mba lenni Yudafɔ Ditiki Sɛɛ nǝmǝ.
translated: When I was young, my father took me to Aba to see his relatives. At that time, we were living in the southern part of the town.

ref: But you will not really be the one speaking. The Spirit from your Father will tell you what to say.
text: Itsyise inni mi loote etiki nnya buidi, bomu mi Anto Onanto eto Lɛwɔnɔ luudi etiki ɔyɛ lǝ mi ǝsuǝ.
translated: If you want to go to the market, tell Anto or Onanto so that they can go with you.

ref: Students are not better than their teacher, and slaves are not better than their master.
text: “Okasebi saa aanso wǝ oteasa. Odomunyǝ saa tsya aanso wǝ onamute.
translated: The lazy one does not eat from the farm. The foolish one does not eat from hunting.

ref: Aren't two sparrows sold for only a penny? But your Father knows when any one of them falls to the ground.
text: Lǝ efi kablɛ kanii, foofo ntsyuɛ nnuǝ boya tsyaa, ke kani kplo embukpǝ lǝ inni Boanto Onanto eto lelabi.
translated: When the chief arrived, everyone gathered together, and he spoke about the importance of unity in the Boanto Onanto festival.

ref: Even the hairs on your head are counted.
text: Nya mimii, Onanto yi mi disi eto sɛwɔni eto leklabi kenke.
translated: My friend, Onanto told me that you should bring the small chair here.

ref: So don't be afraid! You are worth much more than many sparrows. Telling Others about Christ (Luke
text: Nyaso biante sikpi, itsyise befo koya beso baklɔbɛ kpǝ. Kɛnyɛbulǝkǝ Kate Lǝ Yesu Eto Dikiti Sɛɛ Ǝsuǝ Ku Wǝ Bɔni.
translated: Let us love one another, just as God loves us. The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you all.

ref: Don't think I came to bring peace to the earth! I came to bring trouble, not peace.
text: “Biembu biǝ mbǝ diyuǝ kabokokɔ lǝ kawunsiǝ. Owo lo! Mǝmbǝ diyuǝ kabokokɔ, bomu kabia!
translated: Let us all go to the farm together. Hurry up! If you want to go to the farm, get ready!

ref: If you try to save your life, you will lose it. But if you give it up for me, you will surely find it. Rewards (Mark
text: Utsyuǝ saa ke lela nkǝ waata wǝ nkpǝ eto disii, ebonyansǝ mu. Fɛɛ utsyuǝ saa ke loonyansǝ wǝ nkpǝ lǝ mɔ butǝkǝko esoo, wuunyǝ nkpǝ manlo sitinti. Onanto Ebofo Betidi Ba Lefo Yesu Banɔ Kayo.
translated: The child who listens to the word and does not put it into practice is not wise. But the child who hears the word and puts it into practice is like a house built on a rock. This is the word of the Lord Jesus Christ.

ref: Anyone who welcomes a prophet, just because that person is a prophet, will be given the same reward as a prophet. Anyone who welcomes a good person, just because that person is good, will be given the same reward as a good person.
text: Utsyuǝ saa ke loofo Onanto eto unyɛlǝkǝtidi nkǝ uyifo Onanto eto unyɛlǝkǝtidi esoo, ebunyǝ kedikɔ lǝ unyɛlǝkǝtidi eto kayofofo ka woofo lǝ nnya ǝsuǝ. Utsyuǝ saa ke loofo utidi wǝ ni ɔsɛɛ itsyise uyifo ɔsɛɛnyǝ esoo, wǝ ku utidi ǝmǝ ebofo kayofofo itsyi lǝ keyifo ka úyifo eso.
translated: If you want to learn about Onanto, you should ask those who know about Onanto, because only those who know can give you the right information. If you want to do something well, you need to ask those who have done it before, so that you can learn from their experience.

ref: John the Baptist did not go around eating and drinking, and you said, “That man has a demon in him!”
text: Itsyise lefe nǝ Yohanes díbǝǝ, ókle kɛnyɛ nya wǝnnǝ nte. Nya utsyuǝ saa lɛtɛyi nkǝ, ‘Utsyi lɛwɔnɔ bua.’
translated: They sent people to John to ask him who he was. He answered them, ‘I am not the one.’

ref: You people are like children sitting in the market and shouting to each other,
text: “Nya mɔmɔɔ, be muufi mmi eto betidi nkatesǝ ko? Beyifo fe bekpefi ba laakpe kɔyɛ lǝ asi. Lekpa lɔni afa kakpadi mǝ bǝtsyuǝ katɛyi nkǝ,
translated: My friend, can you help me buy some groundnuts? The woman selling them is sitting under the tree over there. Please go with this money and bring me some groundnuts.

ref: But the Son of Man goes around eating and drinking, and you say, “That man eats and drinks too much! He is even a friend of tax collectors and sinners.” Yet Wisdom is shown to be right by what it does. The Unbelieving Towns (Luke
text: Fɛɛ se Utidi Eto Ubi díbǝǝ, údi únǝ. Nya utsyuǝ saa lɛtɛyi nkǝ, ‘Be utidi ǝmfo! Uyifo udikpuu ku utenǝ, mba laafo umǝnko ku babuayifo eto ɔyɛtsyuǝ!’ Fɛɛ utidi eto eyifosa ǝsuǝǝ, nya woǝlǝkǝ Onanto eto kobe ko nkpe wǝ kate nɛ.” Kǝmǝ Kǝtsyuǝ Eto Betidi Mamfo Yesu Banɔ.
translated: When the Evil Spirit leaves a person, it goes around looking for a place to rest but finds none. Then it says, ‘I will go back to my house that I left!’ When it returns, it finds the place clean and put in order. Then it goes and brings seven other spirits more evil than itself, and they all go in and live there. And the final condition of that person is worse than before. That is what will happen to this evil generation.

ref: Yes, Father, this is what pleased you.
text: Ee, Anto, sitinti, nko fála fǝ ibǝ itǝ nɛ.
translated: Yes, Anto, be patient, I will give it to you later.

ref: Then Jesus told the man, “Hold out your hand.” The man did, and it became as healthy as the other one.
text: Mfó nya ɔ́tɛyi osani wǝ eto kɔni díkpǝ nkǝ, “Nǝ fǝ kɔni etsyue.” Únǝ ko útsyue, nya kɔni komǝ disi kótsya wǝ fe kase kunuǝfǝ komǝ nte.
translated: He told the child to go and bring the broom, saying, “Go and bring the broom.” When the child brought it, he used it to sweep the house clean.

ref: When Jesus found out what was happening, he left there and large crowds followed him. He healed all of their sick,
text: Se Yesu lɛnɔ nkǝ bákle kɛnyɛ lǝ wǝ ǝsuǝǝ, údu mfó, nya betidi kpǝ dítǝkǝko wǝ. Ótsya befimfi kenke.
translated: When Jesus saw the crowd coming towards him, he said, these people need something to eat. He asked them to sit down.

ref: but warned them not to tell anyone about him.
text: Nya úti mǝ kusu nkǝ bǝntǝ lǝ betidi lǝ bate bǝ wǝ uni.
translated: If you go, I will give you the thing that you asked for.

ref: “Here is my chosen servant! I love him, and he pleases me. I will give him my Spirit, and he will bring justice to the nations.
text: “Nwǝ mfo ni mɔ uyifoyifo wǝ mínlǝkǝ mintsyǝ, nwǝ maala, nya mǝǝnyǝ disuǝyuǝ lǝ wǝ tinti. Muutǝ mɔ Lɛwɔnɔ lǝ dibǝ disiǝ lǝ wǝ ǝsuǝ, nya woobe mɔ lekpokponkpo letsya utǝ kawunsiǝ kenke.

text: Mimba lɛbɔ bitsyi lǝ bawa eto kafo ǝsuǝ! Asa sɛɛ aambɔ ketsyi lǝ mi kɛnyɛ! Itsyise lesa nǝ diyi mi ketu ǝsuǝǝ, nnya mi kɛnyɛ lǝǝlǝkǝ katɛyi nɛ.

ref: Then about that time Jesus shouted, “Eli, Eli, lema sabachthani?” which means, “My God, my God, why have you deserted me?”
text: Fe abombo ǝtsyǝ etii, Yesu áfa bembembe ɔ́tɛyi nkǝ, “Eli, Eli, lama sabaktani?” Nyamfo eto kasɔ ni, “Mɔ Onanto, mɔ Onanto! Beso fáyani mɛ fǝtsyǝ?”
translated: At about three o’clock, Jesus cried out loudly, “Eli, Eli, lama sabaktani?” This means, “My God, my God! Why have you forsaken me?”

ref: One of them at once ran and grabbed a sponge. He soaked it in wine, then put it on a stick and held it up to Jesus.
text: Mǝ ɔni étsyetsyi nya úfi okosa wǝ bákpe lǝ nte eto sonkpe, nya úfi mu ku uyi úsuko nkǝ lǝ utǝ wǝ lǝ unǝ.
translated: If you want to understand this story well, you have to listen carefully to the words and the meaning.

ref: The officer and the soldiers guarding Jesus felt the earthquake and saw everything else that happened. They were frightened and said, “This man really was God's Son!”
text: Se Omamanyǝ ninǝ ku bamamanyǝ ba díńyǝ bákase Yesu dínyǝ kase kasɔ dítinkǝ ku asa nnya díbǝ mfó kenkee, sikpi ǝmuǝ mǝ tinti! Nya bátɛyi bǝnkǝ, “Sitintii, utidi ǝmfo éyifo Onanto eto Ubi.”
translated: God so loved us that he gave us his only son Jesus to die for us and save us from our sins, so that we may live! The angels said, “Rejoice, for the Savior Christ is born in Bethlehem.”

ref: went and asked for Jesus' body. Pilate gave orders for it to be given to Joseph,
text: Úsu Pilato ɔflɔ nya ɔ́tɔ wǝ nkǝ lǝ ofo Yesu eto ɔkɔ. Pilato ǝtǝ kusu nkǝ befi ɔkɔ ǝmǝ bǝtǝ Yosef.
translated: When Pilate heard this, he ordered that Jesus’ body be given to Joseph.

ref: Suddenly Jesus met them and greeted them. They went near him, held on to his feet, and worshiped him.
text: Lamfolamfoo, Yesu átsyako mǝ, nya ɔ́tɛyi mǝ nkǝ, “Diyuǝ itǝ mi.” Báyɛ bétu wǝ bábokosǝ ǝsuǝ kasɔ bákɔbe wǝ lǝ akpa nya bésumu wǝ.
translated: Afterwards, Jesus called me, and said to me, "Come to me." Then we went and sat down under a tree and talked about our lives.

ref: Jesus and his disciples went to the town of Capernaum. Then on the next Sabbath he went into the synagogue and started teaching.
text: Yesu ku mba ntǝkǝko wǝ ésu bédufǝ umǝ wǝ baakpo Kapernaum. Nya lǝ Yudafɔ eto Ɔnwɛditsyǝyi nǝ ntǝkǝ lǝ mǝ mfó eto busu ǝsuǝ eto kamaa, Yesu ésu Yudafɔ eto katsyakɔ nya útsyiko mǝ Anantotiki bote.
translated: Jesus and his disciples left there and went to Capernaum. When the Jewish festival of Tabernacles was near, Jesus’ brothers said to him, “Leave here and go to Judea, so that your disciples there may see the works you do.”

ref: Everyone was amazed at his teaching. He taught with authority, and not like the teachers of the Law of Moses.
text: Iyifo mba kenke lɛnɔ wǝ asatete nyamǝ yanii, itsyise ɛbɔ lete lǝ mba laate asa itsyi lǝ Yudafɔ eto afi ǝsuǝ. Bomu wǝ asatete eyifo nnya nkpe kalende nya itsyi taa wǝ utu kǝmiǝ.
translated: After the seventh month had come and the Israelites were settled in their towns, the people gathered together as one in Jerusalem. Then the priests began to offer burnt offerings to the Lord according to what is written in the law of Moses.

ref: About that time Jesus came from Nazareth in Galilee, and John baptized him in the Jordan River.
text: Yanklɛ fɛ Yesu ditsyi umǝ wǝ baakpo Nasaret lǝ Galilea eto kasɔ, ubǝ udufǝ, nya Yohanes díyifo wǝ ntuflǝsǝflǝsǝ eto lekpa lǝ Yordan.
translated: After that, Jesus left Nazareth in Galilee and went to John the prophet to be baptized by him in the Jordan river.

ref: A man with leprosy came to Jesus and knelt down. He begged, “You have the power to make me well, if only you wanted to.”
text: Osani utsyuǝ wǝ nlɛ bufisia bufi ǝbǝ Yesu ɔflɔ. Óse akonki ɔ́lɛ wǝ lekpakpa nkǝ lǝ ɔwunsǝko wǝ. Ɔ́tɛyi wǝ nkǝ, “Lǝ alaa, ebofo mɛ botsya nya yuuyuǝ mɛ tsya.”
translated: Jesus and his disciples went to the town of Bethany. He met a man who was sick and lying by the roadside. The man said, "Lord, have mercy on me and heal my sickness."

ref: At once Jesus asked them to come with him. They left their father in the boat with the hired workers and went with him. A Man with an Evil Spirit (Luke
text: Kase Yesu dínyǝ mǝ koo, ókpadi mǝ nkǝ bǝtǝkǝko wǝ. Mǝ tsya átaka akpa akpa báyani mǝ anto Sebedeo ku wǝ beyifoyifo bǝtsyǝ lǝ ɔklɔ ǝmǝ kafo bǝtǝkǝko Yesu. Yesu Ála Lɛwɔnɔ Bua Lǝ Osani Utsyuǝ Ǝsuǝ.
translated: When Jesus was walking by, he saw two brothers. They were casting a net into the lake because they were fishermen. He also saw James and John, the sons of Zebedee, who were in a boat with their father, preparing their nets. Jesus called them. Jesus Calls His First Disciples.

ref: When they got there, Jesus was told that Simon's mother-in-law was sick in bed with fever.
text: Se bésu bédufǝ mfóo, betidi bǝtsyuǝ átɛyi Yesu bǝnkǝ, Simon eto usiǝambe áyila, atikesi ǝmuǝ wǝ olabe.
translated: When they had finished eating, Jesus said to Simon Peter, "Simon son of John, do you love me more than these?"

ref: After Jesus strictly warned the man, he sent him on his way.
text: Mfó nya Yesu lɛtɛyi wǝ nkǝ udu, nya ókpe wǝ kɔfi keklekle nkǝ,
translated: If you believe in Jesus, your heart will be at peace, your life will be full of happiness,

ref: But because of the crowd, they could not get him to Jesus. So they made a hole in the roof above him and let the man down in front of everyone.
text: Nya se banlɛ kusu bunyǝ lǝ bayɛnko wǝ lǝ kesie bedufǝnko wǝ diyo kafo lǝ kase betidi mfui esoo, bésu bábe letsya bǝlǝkǝ ofo nya báyɛnko ufimfi ǝmǝ ku wǝ belabekǝ báyifǝsǝ bǝtsyǝ le Yesu eto akɔ.
translated: If someone offends you, forgive them and do not keep it in your heart, so that your prayers will be answered and you will receive blessings from Jesus.

ref: Jesus answered: The friends of a bridegroom don't go without eating while he is still with them.
text: Yesu ǝtǝ mǝ mbuayɛ lǝ lekpa seka ɔ́tɛyi nkǝ, “Nyalɛ Okafɔsǝ eto bakloboo, bani lesa budi lǝ kusiǝ kakpakɔ kamǝ? Lee, yanle bǝ bǝnyǝ disuǝyuǝ ku okafɔsǝ ǝmǝ nkpe mǝ lentii?
translated: Jesus looked at them and said, “Do you think the shepherd leaves the ninety-nine sheep and goes after the one that is lost? And when he finds it, does he not rejoice more over that one than over the ninety-nine that were not lost?”

ref: One Sabbath Jesus and his disciples were walking through some wheat fields. His disciples were picking grains of wheat as they went along.
text: Lefe lɔni lǝ Ɔnwɛditsyǝyii, se Yesu ku wǝ bakasebi nyɛ lǝ anta eto ɔkuɛ utsyuǝ banlɛ bofee, bǝfǝsǝ anta ǝtsyuǝ balɛ bɔyimi.
translated: But on the third day, as Jesus had said he would rise from the dead, he truly rose from the dead.

ref: Some teachers of the Law of Moses came from Jerusalem and said, “This man is under the power of Beelzebul, the ruler of demons! He is even forcing out demons with the help of Beelzebul.”
text: Nya afi eto bateasa ba ditsyi Yerusalem lɛtɛyi bǝnkǝ, “Lɛwɔnɔ bua ǝbǝ disi lǝ Yesu ǝsuǝ. Nya awɔnɔ bua eto ɔtɔnkotɔnkoo, ni nwǝ dítǝ wǝ ǝsuǝale fɛ ɔnlɛ awɔnɔ bua nyamfó bola kabɔnko lǝ betidi ǝsuǝ.”
translated: Now at that time some people came from Jerusalem saying, “We have seen what Jesus has done. Some people are amazed, but others say he is only pretending to be good and deceiving the people.”

ref: I promise you that any of the sinful things you say or do can be forgiven, no matter how terrible those things are.
text: “Nlɛ mi bɔtɛyi sitinti minkǝ Onanto ebufi lebua saa nǝ betidi díyifo ku diye bua saa nǝ baakpadi Onanto ɔtsyɛ.
translated: If you want to understand the story of Onanto, you must listen to the elders and the wise people who know about Onanto well.

ref: But if you speak against the Holy Spirit, you can never be forgiven. That sin will be held against you forever.
text: Fɛɛ diye bua saa nǝ utidi lookpadi Onanto eto Lɛwɔnɔ Kɛnkɛɛ, Onanto ǝmbufi ni ɔtsyɛ wǝ kulefe. Itsyise wuufi lebua nǝmfo fenkǝ lebua nǝ wembotasuǝ.”
translated: If you go to the house of the chief called Onanto in Lewono Kenke, Onanto will greet you and give you a place to sit. You must greet him first before he greets you.

ref: Anyone who obeys God is my brother or sister or mother.”
text: Utsyuǝ saa wǝ leeyifo Onanto eto lelabii, wǝ ni mɔ ɔyimisani nye mɔ ɔyimisio, nye mɔ ambe nɛ.”
translated: When someone is doing Onanto work for the community, they say “this is our tradition and our custom, and it is for all of us.”

ref: He used stories to teach them many things, and this is part of what he taught:
text: Lefe kpǝ lǝ wǝ asatete ǝsuǝǝ, údi etiki ɔ́yɛnko lǝ akpa ǝsuǝ lǝ ufi ulǝkǝ asa nnya óla nkǝ loote mǝ kasɔ. Asa nnya óte mǝ ni nkǝ.
translated: If you want to do something good, you must first think about it and then do it so that people will see that you are good. Do not do it just to be seen.

ref: The seeds that fell along the road are the people who hear the message. But Satan soon comes and snatches it away from them.
text: Betidi bǝtsyuǝ ale kase eyibibi ǝtsyuǝ luubǝ ekpo lǝ kusu ɔflɔ. Lǝ kase banlɛ bɔnɔɔ, ete Obonsam ǝǝbǝ nya wǝǝnwǝ nnya kǝlǝkǝ lǝ mǝ nsusu ǝsuǝ.
translated: Long ago, there was a woman who had three children and they lived in a small village. In that same village, it was believed that the Devil would come at night to take away children who did not listen to their parents.

ref: It is like what happens when a mustard seed is planted in the ground. It is the smallest seed in all the world.
text: Ite fenkǝ utidi éfi diyibibi nǝ nso bɔfɛ lǝ kawunsiǝ úsu óto.
translated: If you want to succeed in life, you must work hard and be honest.

ref: But once it is planted, it grows larger than any garden plant. It even puts out branches that are big enough for birds to nest in its shade. The Reason for Teaching with Stories (Matthew
text: Lefe ditsyuǝ eto kamaa, diǝkǝ kafiani kuyi. Nya nkomǝ eto kuyi komfo laamɔ kaso ɔkuɛ eto ediyi fɛfɛ kenke. Koata kalɛ kpǝ. Nya baklɔbɛ laato ayo lǝ kuyi komǝ eto kalɛ ǝsuǝ.”
translated: If someone wants to travel, they should prepare well. The elders say that good preparation makes the journey smooth. Listen carefully. The ancestors always advised us to prepare well before any journey.

ref: He did not tell them anything without using stories. But when he was alone with his disciples, he explained everything to them. A Storm (Matthew
text: Etiki saa wuudi mǝǝ, woatɛyi nnya kayɛ lǝ akpa ǝsuǝ. Lǝ lefe nǝ itǝ kusu bǝ wonko wǝ bakasebi bamǝ hã sii, nya fɛ wǝǝlǝkǝ nnya kasɔ kate mǝ nɛ. Yesu Éti Ufiebi Eto Ǝsuale Kusu.
translated: If you believe in him, you will not walk in darkness. He is the one who brings light to all people, so that everyone who follows him will have the light of life. Jesus is the Light of the World.

ref: Jesus was in the back of the boat with his head on a pillow, and he was asleep. His disciples woke him and said, “Teacher, don't you care that we're about to drown?”
text: Lǝ lefe nǝmfóo, Yesu ǝsiǝ lǝ ɔklɔ ǝmǝ eto kama, úfi wǝ disi útǝkǝ lǝ esunde ǝsuǝ nya ɔnlɛ selabe. Nyaso básanklisǝ wǝ nya bátɛyi wǝ bǝnkǝ, “Oteasa, ɛnlɛ fǝ bulǝ bǝnkǝ boǝbǝ bukpǝ?”
translated: When they had finished eating, Jesus asked Simon Peter, "Simon, son of John, do you love me more than these?" He answered, "Yes, Lord, you know that I love you."

ref: When the man saw Jesus in the distance, he ran up to him and knelt down.
text: Utidi ǝmfo ǝńyǝ kɔsɛsɛ fɛ únyǝ Yesu. Nyaso útsyetsyi úsu óse akonki lǝ Yesu eto ǝnǝmi.
translated: If you believe in Jesus, then you will receive the blessings that come from Jesus.

ref: The men taking care of the pigs ran to the town and the farms to spread the news. Then the people came out to see what had happened.
text: Nya mba nlɛ bakplako bamǝ bobee, dítsyetsyi bésu umǝ ǝmǝ ku mba lɛkɛ lǝ wǝ ɔflɔ mfó, nya bésu batɛyi betidi ba kenke lesa nǝ díbǝ. Mba bátɛyi ésu mfó, bésu bábe lesa nǝ díbǝ.
translated: If you want to cook corn dough for porridge, first you put water in a pot and place it on the fire, then you stir the dough until it becomes thick. When it becomes thick, you continue to stir until it is done.

ref: When they came to Jesus, they saw the man who had once been full of demons. He was sitting there with his clothes on and in his right mind, and they were terrified.
text: Nya lefe nǝ bǝbǝ Yesu ɔflɔɔ, bǝnyǝ utidi wǝ awɔnɔ bua kpǝ dísiǝ lǝ ǝsuǝ mfó. Nya únsi lǝ kasɔ, ónkpe awu, íkakpa ko wǝ. Nya sikpi dímuǝ mǝmblɛ kenke.
translated: When they brought Jesus out, they made him carry his own cross to the place called Skull. There they crucified him, and two others with him. Jesus was in the middle.

ref: But the people laughed at him. After Jesus had sent them all out of the house, he took the girl's father and mother and his three disciples and went to where she was.
text: Se ɔ́tɛyi nkoo, betidi ba ńyǝ mfó étsyiko wǝ bumǝ. Nyaso Yesu ǝtǝ nkǝ mǝmblɛ bɔ besu lekple, nya útǝ ubi ǝmǝ eto anto ku ambe ku wǝ bakasebi itsyǝ bamǝ nkǝ bedufǝ kaka ubi ǝmǝ díkpe ónlabe lǝ kasɔ.
translated: If you believe only, you will see the glory of God. That is what Jesus said to the people when he raised Lazarus, so that everyone would know that God has power to do anything and to help those who trust in him.

ref: While Jesus was still speaking, some people came from Jairus' home and said, “Your daughter has died! Why bother the teacher anymore?”
text: Lǝ lefe nǝ Yesu nlɛ nyamfo bɔtɛyii, batɔnkpe bǝtsyuǝ étsyi Yudafɔ eto katsyakɔ kamǝ eto uninǝ ǝmǝ eto diyo bǝbǝ nya bátɛyi wǝ bǝnkǝ, “Fǝ ubi ǝmǝ ébukpǝ koko. Beso fensi fanlɛ Oteasa ǝmfo bulǝ?”
translated: They brought Jesus from Caiaphas to the governor’s headquarters. It was early morning. They themselves did not enter the headquarters, so that they would not be defiled, but could eat the Passover. So Pilate went outside to them and said, “What accusation do you bring against this man?”

ref: But Jesus said, “Prophets are honored by everyone, except the people of their hometown and their relatives and their own family.”
text: Yesu átɛyi mǝ nkǝ, “Onanto eto unyǝlǝkǝtidi kpe dibu lǝ nlebe kpǝ. Fɛɛ lǝ wǝ kosate eto umǝǝ, lǝ wǝ betidi ntii, ku lǝ wǝ kosate eto diyo eto betidi eto ǝnǝmii, umbǝ dibu saa.”
translated: Jesus answered him, “Truly, truly, I say to you, unless one is born of water and the Spirit, he cannot enter the kingdom of God. That which is born of the flesh is flesh, and that which is born of the Spirit is spirit.”

ref: But when Herod heard about Jesus, he said, “This must be John! I had his head cut off, and now he has come back to life.”
text: Lefe nǝ Herodes lɛnɔ nyamfóo, wuntsya átɛyi nkǝ, “Yohanes wǝ útǝ bála disi uni disi ósankli utsyi ɔkɔmǝ úbǝ!”
translated: When Herod heard this, he said, “John, whom I beheaded, has been raised from the dead!”

ref: After the apostles returned to Jesus, they told him everything they had done and taught.
text: Batɔnkpe ba Yesu lékpe nkǝ besu batsyilama lekpake mfóo, ésinkli bǝbǝ bátɛyi wǝ nnya kenke bésu beyifo ku asa nnya báte.
translated: The disciples of Jesus gathered together and discussed among themselves about what they had heard and seen, and they wondered about the things he had done.

ref: Isn't he the carpenter, the son of Mary? Aren't James, Joseph, Judas, and Simon his brothers? Don't his sisters still live here in our town?” The people were upset because of what he was doing.
text: Inni wǝ ni Maria eto ubi wǝ ni nwǝ laawe keyi? Lee inni wǝ ni Yakobo ku Yose ku Yuda ku Simon eto ɔyimisani? Nya wǝ bayimisio tsya nkpe mfo!” Nyaso betidi bamfo mámfo wǝ.
translated: Isn't this the carpenter, the son of Mary and the brother of James, Joseph, Judas, and Simon? Aren't his sisters here with us?” And they took offense at him.

ref: I swear that I will give you as much as half of my kingdom, if you want it.”
text: Nya usi ɔ́tɛyi wǝ tsya nkǝ, “Ekpe lǝ mɔ lekankpomɛ, minkǝ muutǝ fǝ lesa nǝ ke fɔɔtɔ mɛ, lǝ mɔ sekakedikɔ eto kutsyǝ kulaa tsya!”
translated: Then the woman said to her child, "If you do not listen to my words, I will take you to the place where the ogre lives, and he will eat you up!"

ref: The girl left and asked her mother, “What do you think I should ask for?” Her mother answered, “The head of John the Baptist!”
text: Nyaso usiobi ǝmfo átaka úsu ɔ́tɔ wǝ ambe nkǝ, “Be ele bǝ ntɔ?” Nya wǝ ambe lɛtɛyi wǝ nkǝ, “Tɔ wǝ Yohanes Ntuflǝsǝtidi eto disi.”
translated: Jesus asked them again, “What do you want?” They replied, “Grant that we may sit, one at your right and one at your left, in your glory.”

ref: They left in a boat for a place where they could be alone.
text: Nyaso bésu bédufǝ lǝ ɔklɔ lǝ besu kaka betidi lǝmbǝ.
translated: The woman is sweeping the room with a long broom.

ref: Jesus told his disciples to tell the people to sit down on the green grass.
text: Yesu átɛyi wǝ bakasebi nkǝ bǝtǝ lǝ betidi bamǝ kenke lǝ bǝsiǝ kasɔ lǝ sefa siamǝ ǝsuǝ mfó lǝ etsyukpa etsyukpa ǝsuǝ.
translated: Jesus told the people that they should always pray and not give up.

ref: Jesus' disciples picked up twelve large baskets of leftover bread and fish.
text: Nya se wǝ bakasebi lɛsɛ didisa nǝ díbu bakɔɔ, díyi lefosi etsyitsya kplɛ ǝnuǝ.
translated: If you want to succeed in life, you must work hard and be honest.

ref: He could see that the disciples were struggling hard, because they were rowing against the wind. Not long before morning, Jesus came toward them. He was walking on the water and was about to pass the boat.
text: Únyǝ nkǝ wǝ betidi édufǝ lǝ bulǝ ǝsuǝ ku kase bánlɛ ɔklɔ ǝmǝ bofo bonwa itsyise ufiebi lɛ bɔsɔ kǝlǝ mǝ. Nyaso lǝ kolensiǝnto kplɛɛ, Yesu áyɛ lǝ ntu bomǝ ǝsuǝ úsu mǝ ɔflɔ eti nkǝ lǝ ɔyɛ ofe.
translated: When you are in trouble or in need, do not be afraid to ask for help from those who love you. Just as Jesus loves us, we should also love and help one another.

ref: When the disciples saw Jesus walking on the water, they thought he was a ghost, and they started screaming.
text: Nya se bǝnyǝ wǝ lǝ ntu bomǝ ǝsuǝǝ, báfa bátɛyi bǝnkǝ, “Ɔkɔ ni.”
translated: When they arrived at the riverbank, the elders said, "Let us go."

ref: As soon as they got out of the boat, the people recognized Jesus.
text: Kase báyifǝ lǝ ɔklɔ ǝmǝ koo, betidi ǝtsyǝ Yesu lekpo.
translated: When they arrived at the house, they saw Jesus inside.

ref: You are good at rejecting God's commands so that you can follow your own teachings!
text: Yesu esi ɔ́tɛyi mǝ nkǝ, “Bekpe awɛsu ǝtsyuǝ nnya biefi akayɛ lǝ nnya ǝsuǝ kani Onanto eto kɔfi budi, keyifo batekoko ku banakoko eto akpa.
translated: Jesus answered them, "Truly, truly I say to you, unless a grain of wheat falls into the earth and dies, it remains alone; but if it dies, it bears much fruit."

ref: The Pharisees and many others obey the teachings of their ancestors. They always wash their hands in the proper way before eating.
text: Bǝnyǝ nyamfo bǝtsyǝ lekpo itsyise Farisifɔ ku Yudafɔ fɛfɛ kenke eeyifo lǝ mǝ batekoko, banakoko eto akpa nnya báyani bǝtsyǝ nya báte mǝ ǝsuǝ.
translated: Some friends brought a man to Jesus who was being followed by Pharisees and teachers of the law, and they wanted to see what he would do for the sick man.

ref: The Pharisees and teachers asked Jesus, “Why don't your disciples obey what our ancestors taught us to do? Why do they eat without washing their hands?”
text: Lǝ nyaso Farisifɔ ku afi eto bateasa átɔ Yesu bǝnkǝ, “Beso fǝ mba ntǝkǝko fǝ lǝǝnyifo lǝ bo batekoko ku banakoko eto akpa ǝsuǝ? Nya banlɛ asa budi lǝ mǝ ani mǝflǝmflǝmflǝ ǝsuǝ?”
translated: Then some Pharisees and teachers of the law came to Jesus and asked, “Why do your disciples break the tradition of the elders? They don’t wash their hands before they eat!”

ref: But you let people get by without helping their parents when they should. You let them say that what they own has been offered to God.
text: Fɛɛ biatɛyi biǝnkǝ ɛlɛkɛ bǝ utidi ebofo lesa bɔni butǝ wǝ anto ku ambe ba lesa lehiã, ku nsusu bǝ lesa nǝmǝ eyifo kɛnkɛ, kato taflatsyǝ ku nsusu bǝ wuufi lesa nǝmǝ utǝ Onanto bomu eso.
translated: If someone does not respect the elders, he will not receive blessings, and even if he has children, they will not prosper, but if he respects the elders, he will receive blessings from God.
